messages.content: me and jordan broke up last weekend and i'm just kinda sad about it

That kind of sadness makes sense — even when a breakup is the right thing, there's still a real loss in it.

How long were you two together?
it was pretty mutual, nobody did anything terrible, we just wanted different things

Those can actually be some of the harder ones to sit with — no clear villain, no obvious reason to be angry, just the quiet ache of something that didn't fit.

What's been the heaviest part of it for you this week?
still though, three years. i keep reaching for my phone to text them stuff and then remembering

That reflex is one of the most disorienting parts — your habits haven't caught up to the new reality yet. Three years builds a lot of those small moments.

What kind of things have you been reaching to send?
i had a little cry tonight watching one of our old shows. felt good actually, like letting it out
There's something honest about that kind of cry — it's not falling apart, it's just feeling what's true. Sounds like you're letting yourself grieve it rather than pushing it away.
i'll be ok, i know i will. just gonna be a sad few weeks and that's normal i think
That sounds like a pretty clear-eyed way to hold it. Sad and okay at the same time — those aren't opposites.
